title: Correcting a Faulty Primality Test using Miller-Rabin Algorithm

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a single integer n (1 <= n <= 10000)
statement.outputFormat: Output is a boolean value (true if the number is prime, false otherwise)

hints.text: Review the Miller-Rabin Primality Test algorithm to understand the logic behind the function

editorial.title: Miller-Rabin Primality Test
editorial.intuition: The Miller-Rabin primality test is a probabilistic algorithm used to determine whether a given number is prime or composite.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to fix the existing Miller-Rabin Primality Test implementation to correctly check if a number is prime.
commonMistakes: Incorrect implementation of the power function
